verses.commentary: Settle there. Build altar. To God who appeared when fleeing Esau. Return to the place of the first vision (chapter 28). Reset. Renewal.
Purify yourselves. Change clothes. Rachel's stolen idols (31:19) and plunder from Shechem must go. Preparation for holy ground.
Jacob testifies to God's faithfulness.
Rings in ears (likely amulets). Permanent removal.
Set out. Terror of God fell on towns. No one pursued. God shields them from retaliation. Supernatural fear.
Jacob and all the people with him came to Luz (that is, Bethel) in the land of Canaan.
God revealed himself here.
Deborah died. Rebekah's nurse. Buried under oak. Brief but poignant. Her presence suggests Rebekah may have died (not recorded). Family transition.
God appeared to Jacob again. Blessed him. Second divine encounter at Bethel. Confirmation and blessing.
Formal name change. Confirming what happened at Peniel (32:28). God ratifies the new identity.
I am God Almighty (El Shaddai, אֵל שַׁדַּי). Be fruitful. Increase. The Abrahamic blessing fully confirmed to Israel. Royal line promised.
Third element of covenant: the land. Full covenant transfer complete.
The theophany ends. God departs from the place of encounter.
Like his first visit (28:18). Consecration.
Bethel is confirmed as sacred site.
Great difficulty. Ominous.
Rachel wanted another son (30:24).

She was dying. Jacob renamed him Benjamin (בִּנְיָמִין), son of the right hand or son of the south. Jacob transforms sorrow into blessing. Rachel dies in childbirth, tragic fulfillment of Jacob's curse (31:32).
Rachel died. Her tomb becomes landmark (1 Samuel 10:2).
The pillar endures. Rachel weeps for her children (Jeremiah 31:15).
Beyond Migdal Eder (tower of the flock).
Shocking betrayal. Reuben (firstborn) sleeps with father's concubine. Later cursed for this (49:3-4).
Six sons. The twelve tribes begin to be formalized.
Sons of Rachel. Two sons. Joseph and Benjamin.
Sons of Bilhah. Two sons.
Sons of Zilpah. Two sons. Twelve sons total.
In Mamre, near Hebron. Return to family land. Three generations converge.
Long life. Twenty years more than Abraham (175).
Isaac died. Peaceful death.
